videos.title: Uncovering Nairobi Dark Side | Walking Through the Most Dangerous Downtown Streets | Full Tour 2025
videos.description:
Uncovering Nairobi Dark Side | Walking Through the Most Dangerous Downtown Streets | Full Tour 2025

In this video, we uncover Nairobi’s dark side as we walk through some of the most dangerous streets in downtown Nairobi. This raw and uncut walking tour exposes the real atmosphere, hidden alleys, street hustlers, and risky zones that most visitors never explore. Experience the true sounds, tension, and daily struggles of people living and working in these high-crime areas.

This documentary-style tour is for awareness and education, showing the survival hustle, fast street life, and the unseen reality of Nairobi’s toughest neighborhoods. If you enjoy urban exploration, Kenyan street documentaries, and raw city walking tours, don’t forget to like, comment, and subscribe for more videos.

#Nairobi #NairobiKenya #NairobiWalkingTour #DangerousStreets #KenyaDocumentary #AfricanCities #UrbanExploration #TravelAfrica #DowntownNairobi

videos.title: Raw & Uncut Walking Through Most Dangerous Neighborhood in Nairobi, Kenya | Full Documentary 2026
videos.description:
Raw & Uncut Walking Through Most Dangerous Neighborhood in Nairobi, Kenya | Full Documentary 2026

In this video, we take you on a raw and uncut walking documentary through one of the most dangerous and feared neighborhoods in Nairobi, Kenya 🇰🇪. Filmed in real time with no narration, no filters, and no staged moments, this full documentary captures the true street life of Nairobi in 2026. Walk through tense streets and hidden corners where everyday survival, struggle, and resilience unfold naturally. Far away from tourist attractions and glossy city images, this journey exposes the real Nairobi rarely shown by mainstream media. From intense atmospheres to unexpected human moments, the camera reveals urban realities as they are. If you’re interested in dangerous walking tours, urban exploration, African documentaries, real street footage, and unfiltered city life, this video is a must-watch. Watch till the end for a powerful, immersive, and unforgettable Nairobi street experience.

#Nairobi #Kenya #RawDocumentary #DangerousNeighborhood #WalkingTour #StreetLife #UrbanExploration #AfricaDocumentary #NairobiStreets #Travel2026

videos.title: Is NAIROBI The BEST Next City To Visit? | Lonely Planet's Best in Travel 2024
videos.description:
Follow us as the dreamers and doers of one of Africa's most vibrant cities reveal the sights, sounds, tastes, smells, and feelings that Nairobi gives you.  Where else can you go to a carnival festival, night clubbing, and a wildlife safari all within one city?  Find out why Kenya's capital is the epicenter of cool on the African continent.

Executive Producer: Annie Greenberg
Senior Producer: Matt Paco
Director of Photography: Jack Pearce
Local Producer/Host: Brian Msafiri
Writer/Host: Neema Githere
Video Editor: Max Dickson
Camera Operator: Denis Mwangi

See all 50 places on our 2024 Best in Travel list here: https://www.lonelyplanet.com/best-in-travel?utm_source=youtube&utm_medium=social&utm_campaign=bit_youtube

#lonelyplanet #bestintravel #nairobi #kenya
videos.transcript: I'm Brian Msafiri, and I grew up here in Nairobi. I work as a producer. And trust me, being in Nairobi is a full sensory experience. You're going to meet the dreamers and doers who call this city home. And we'll show you the sights, smells, tastes and sounds of Nairobi. Nairobi looks like the rainbow. Like a beauty that you can't describe, you know. The graffiti you see on the street. It's like a painting. Vibrant people. Everybody got some style. Nairobi looks like this... ...like a national park right in the city. Nairobi looks like two white rhinos in the morning, and it just took me 10 minutes to get here from my house. How epic is that? Look! Considered the world's only wildlife capital, Nairobi National Park is only fenced in on three sides, allowing for a natural migration to and from the surrounding plains. At Sheldrick Wildlife Trust, nearby visitors can get to feed and pet rescued baby elephants. It's beautiful, isn't it? My name is Patricia, and I'm going to show you some of the art, the fashion, the sights that the city has to offer. Come with me. I'll show you. This is the Opportunity Factory. Now, some of these things are items that you would find in open-air markets in Nairobi. You'd find by the roadside. And them bringing it here together means that you can actually get to see some of the artisans creating these items right here. And this space is the Kuona art space, where we've got folks who are exploring streetwear, who are exploring street art. Who are exploring installations using materials that they find all over the place that represent Nairobi in all its dopeness. On the weekend, Nairobi looks like Uhuru Park. Games, rides, fun. Everyone actually kind of just gets to come together and have fun. In Nairobi, you'll see matatus everywhere. Matatus are like mini buses, but the thing about matatus is the art and graffiti on them. They're so unique to Nairobi. You're not going to find them anywhere else in the world. Hi! I’m Ann, and I hope you're hungry. Let's eat. Nairobi tastes like... It's got a rich flavor to it. A mix of different spices. Chili and salt to add the flavor. It's bittersweet. The junk and the street food, yeah they are banging, bro. It tastes like a fruit. Nairobi tastes like the mix of crisp kale and sweet potato in Mama Rock's iconic burgers. Nairobi tastes like hints of cardamom in your tea and spices that speak to the Indian influence on the Kenyan cuisine. Nairobi tastes like a nice smoky on a Sunday afternoon, man. So this is a smoky. It's more like a rendition of a small hotdog without the bun. And that only costs 20 cents! And Nairobi also tastes like warm chapatis. And here at Bridges Organic, they make theirs with pumpkin and sometimes carrot. And it's like a modern, organic take on a traditional dish. So let’s try it out. Nairobi tastes like some of the best restaurants in the world. Personally, I love Cultiva. It's got a wide range of amazing dishes. Everything's kind of grown on the same space that you get to eat it from. Very cozy, homey. My favorite dish is the pork belly tacos. I don't know the kind of veggies they use, but that thing slaps, okay? I’d drive a whole 3 hours just to come have this taco. Now, one of the dishes you should try out when you're here is ugali. This is a staple food in the country, so let's dig in. For the full Kibanda experience, don't be shy to use your hands. Nairobi smells... Inhale. Woo! These are the smells of Nairobi. Nairobi smells like wet soil after it's rained. You can smell the rain. Smells like fresh lavender. Burnt rubber. Fresh flowers, a bit of exhaust fumes, fresh and just, like, sweaty. It smells like more trees than you can imagine. Nairobi smells like woodwork. The smell of wood. What's good! This is Blinky Bill, I’m a musician from Nairobi City. Welcome. This place gets kind of loud sometimes. And these are the sounds of my city. Nairobi sounds like the club on a Friday night. It's like a music that you want to dance to. Nairobi sounds like some of the best nights in the world. Muze, for example. We don't even know when the sun comes up, but we’re there. It's one of the few clubs that goes for that long. And at least once in your life, you need to give yourself that. One thing you'll hear in Kenya is the sound of drums. That's the sound that I really like. It’s the sound of one of the tribes here. Think it’s a Maasai tribe. And you'll just have to come to Kenya to hear some of these sounds. Nairobi is loud. Nairobi is intense. But then again, Nairobi is love. Nairobi feels like a warm hug. You will feel the love. Nairobi also feels like nature. My name is Neema Githere and I'm one of the co-authors of the Lonely Planet Kenya Guidebook. You've experienced all of the other senses, but really, Nairobi is a feeling. Nairobi feels like the breeze at Karura forest. Karura Forest is one of my favorite refuges in the city. Every time I'm here, I just feel a sense of peace. Incredibly meditative. Away from the hustle and bustle of the rest of the city. Oh, Nairobi has, like, a lot of texture. Nairobi, to me, feels like beadwork. I've been coming to the Maasai Market for as long as I can remember, but these beads in particular always stand out to me because I get to watch her make them in live time. And she just like has this precision about all of her artwork and the designs. Like, each of the different kinds of necklaces and headpieces correspond to something within the culture, within the Maa culture. It's almost like a massage for your fingertips. All of the tiny pieces coming together to create this like, collage of texture. And that's what driving around the city feels like. You have skyscrapers and all of this traffic. There’s still at the heart of it, this texture of culture. Nairobi feels like home. Nairobi feels like home. It does feel like home. It's like a second home to anyone who is visiting. And you'll feel it when you come here. Thanks for watching Lonely Planet. Subscribe to see more videos like this from all across the world.

videos.title: The PERFECT Kenya Itinerary - Safari adventure and Kenya travel tips
videos.description:
Come with me on the perfect Kenya itinerary! We're going on an epic safari through Tsavo National Park, visit Nairobi and explore the stunning beaches of Watamu. 

🪽 This video is sponsored by SafetyWing, and you can find out about their amazing Nomad Insurance policy here: https://bit.ly/3YdEfBb

🇰🇪  If you feel inspired to visit Kenya, check out the Kenya tourism website here: https://bit.ly/4er5eyR

🏨  The hotels we stayed in:

Kilaguni Serena Safari Lodge: https://bit.ly/483p6Wt
Soroi Lions Bluff Lodge: https://bit.ly/3zYTdSg
Medina Palms Watamu: https://bit.ly/3BGNGjM

🥩  Eat at Ankole Grill in Nairobi: https://bit.ly/4eBFKyN
🌳  Go on a zip-line adventure in Nairobi:  https://bit.ly/4eHbsLa
🐢  Volunteer at the turtle sanctuary in Watamu: https://bit.ly/3zPw0lr
🌅  Visit Lichthaus in Watamu: https://bit.ly/4ewBfpa
⛴️  Go on a Dhow Cruise: https://bit.ly/40arGrw

Subscribe to my channel for more travel vlogs and worldwide travel tips: https://www.youtube.com/subscribe_widget?p=sandymakessense

You can see my international travel playlist here: https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLeLAxZdQm9DyjBpcLoVzJ3qNO8Xt1xGEV

Follow me here:

Instagram: http://instagram.com/SandyMakesSense
TikTok: https://www.tiktok.com/@sandymakessense
Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/SandyMakesSense
Blog: https://www.sandymakessense.com/

If you're looking for Kenya travel tips or for inspiration on things to do in Kenya, I've got you! This was the first time I ever visited Kenya and it was absolutely magical. Going on safari in Kenya is something I will absolutely never forget and it's truly a bucket list destination 🧳 ✈️ 🐘 🦁 🇰🇪 

#MagicalKenya #Kenya #KenyaSafari

videos.title: Nairobi Top 10 Best Neighborhoods to Live. 2023
videos.description:
Nairobi is the capital city of Kenya and is known for its diverse cultures, beautiful landscapes, and bustling city life. If you are looking for a place to call home in Nairobi, it's essential to find a neighborhood that suits your lifestyle and provides a safe and secure environment.

Whether you are an expat, a wealthy Kenyan, or a family looking for a serene and upscale residential area, Nairobi has plenty of neighborhoods to choose from. From the bustling commercial and residential area of Westlands to the exclusive and secluded suburb of Runda, each neighborhood has its unique features and charm.

In this list, we have compiled the top 10 best and most secure neighborhoods to live in Nairobi. These neighborhoods are popular among expats, wealthy Kenyans, and families due to their exclusivity, serenity, and proximity to social amenities.

Follow us:
https://www.facebook.com/UrbanGreenRC/
https://urbangreenrealtykenya.com/
https://jiji.co.ke/shop/urbangreenrealty
https://instagram.com/urbangreen_real...
+254724756773

exclusivity Serenity and proximity to social amenities stay tuned to see which is the most sought after suburb in Nairobi at number one number 10 kilimani kilimani is a vibrant and diverse neighborhood located in the Southeastern part of Nairobi the neighborhood is popular among expats young professionals and families due to its close proximity to the city center and a wide range of social amenities kilimani is home to several modern apartments luxurious townhouses and high-end Villas the neighborhood is known for its Lively nightlife with plenty of bars restaurants and cafes kilimani is also home to shopping centers like the yaya Center and Prestige Plaza as well as several schools and Hospitals number nine killaleshwa kilaleshwa is an upscale neighborhood located in the western part of Nairobi just a few kilometers from the city center the neighborhood is known for its luxurious residential homes modern apartments and Serene environment kilaleshwe is home to several high-end restaurants bars and cafes as well as shopping centers like the kazuku center and the Valley Arcade the neighborhood is also in close proximity to social amenities such as hospitals schools and recreational facilities kilaleshwa is a desirable neighborhood for expats young professionals and families looking for a Serene and upscale residential area number eight Westlands Westlands is a lively and Cosmopolitan neighborhood situated in the Northwestern part of Nairobi it is a popular commercial and residential area with a wide range of social amenities making it a desirable location for expats young professionals and families Westlands is known for its bustling nightlife high-end shopping malls like sarit Center and Westgate Mall and in upscale restaurants and cafes the neighborhood is home to several International organizations it is also in close proximity to social amenities such as hospitals schools and recreational facilities with its diverse culture and vibrant atmosphere Westlands offers a dynamic and exciting lifestyle to its residents number seven Kitty suru Kitty suru is a residential suburb located about 10 kilometers Northwest of nairobi's central business district the neighborhood is characterized by large upscale homes many of which are owned by high net worth individuals and expatriates the properties in kirisuru are typically spacious with well-manicured Gardens swimming pools and and other high-end amenities the area is also known for its Lush Greenery and Serene surroundings making it a popular destination for those seeking a peaceful and exclusive place to call home number six Spring Valley Spring Valley is an exclusive residential neighborhood located in the Northwestern part of Nairobi the neighborhood is known for its luxurious homes quiet streets and beautiful gardens Spring Valley is popular among expats and Wealthy Kenyans due to its exclusivity and proximity to social amenities such as malls restaurants and schools the schools shopping centers and recreational facilities the area is considered very safe and is a popular choice for high net worth individuals number three is a leafy upscale suburb located approximately seven kilometers north of nairobi's central business district the neighborhood is known for its luxurious homes tranquil atmosphere and Lush Greenery mataiga is home to some of nairobi's most prominent residents including high-ranking government officials diplomats and business people the area is characterized by large well-manicured properties with sprawling Lawns swimming pools and other upscale amenities in addition to its prestigious Residential Properties mataiga is home to the mataga golf club one of the oldest and most prestigious golf clubs in Kenya the club offers an 18-hole championship course as well as a range of other sports and recreational facilities number two Karen Karen is a leafy upscale suburb located in the southwestern part of Nairobi it is named after Karen blixen the Danish author who wrote out of Africa and who lived in the area for many years Karen is known for its large luxurious homes beautiful gardens and Serene surroundings the neighborhood is popular among expatriates diplomats and Wealthy Kenyans who are seeking a peaceful and exclusive place to live in addition to its prestigious Residential Properties Karen is home to several attractions including the Karen blixen Museum the garage Center and the Nairobi National Park which is located on its Southern border number one jijiri jujiri is a Suburban neighborhood located in the northern part of Nairobi Kenya it is known for being the location of the United Nations Headquarters in Africa as well as several other International organizations and diplomatic missions as a result yujiri is a highly Cosmopolitan area that attracts a large number of expatriates in international professionals the neighborhood is characterized by wide tree-lined streets and spacious properties many of which are owned by diplomats and high-ranking government officials gujiri is one of the more exclusive neighborhoods in Nairobi with properties that feature modern architectural designs high-end finishes and top of the line amenities in addition to its prestigious Residential Properties gigiri is home to several international schools including the International School of Kenya and the Rosslyn Academy the neighborhood also offers a range of other amenities including shopping centers restaurants and recreational facilities one of the main attractions of jijiri is the United Nations complex which includes several buildings and Facilities that house various un agencies and departments the complex is located on a large well-maintained campus that includes beautiful gardens fountains and sculptures the United Nations complex is a major employer in the area with thousands of people working there each day jijiri is also home to the Village Market one of nairobi's largest and most popular shopping centers The Village Market features a range of international and local stores as well as restaurants cafes and a cinema the shopping center is a popular destination for both residents and visitors to the area overall jajiri is a highly sought after neighborhood in Nairobi known for its exclusivity Cosmopolitan atmosphere and international flavor it is a popular destination for expatriates diplomats and high net worth individuals seeking a secure and convenient place to live there are many other great neighborhoods that deserve to be on this list but due to several reasons such as size popularity and also proximity to some of the mentioned neighborhoods have not made the cut neighborhoods such as the Geary loresho Hyuna and rossly to mention just a few there you have it nairobi's best 10 neighborhoods Please Subscribe for more informative and entertaining videos [Music] thank you [Music] [Music] thank you [Music] [Music] thank you

safetyNotes: If you are looking for a place to call home in Nairobi, it's essential to find a neighborhood that suits your lifestyle and provides a safe and secure environment
recommendations: Nairobi Top 10 Best Neighborhoods to Live
In this list, we have compiled the top 10 best and most secure neighborhoods to live in Nairobi

videos.transcript: Hi guys. So, welcome back to my channel. So, on my last video, which got quite a few uh attention, I think it's like in one day about 16,000 views and still growing. Uh there was a lot of comments that came up about safety in Kenya. So, that's probably one of the most common questions that I saw in the comments in particular about Nairobi because I have not been to Mombasa yet. That'll be soon and then I can talk about that as well. Some people say it's dangerous here in Nairobi. Others say it's fine. And then you got the comments online acting like you'll get robbed, kidnapped, or sick the second you land. So, in this video, I'm going to give you the real travel reality of Nairobi, crime, scams, and health, which includes HIV, which seemed to be pretty common in the questions uh in in the last video. So, I'm going to try to do it without any kind of fear-mongering, but at the same time also without sugar coating anything. This is based on my own experiences, not headlines [music] or internet panic. So, stick around for today's video. [music] Hang [music] night. [music] >> First, let's be clear about something. Kenya is not Europe. It's not Japan. It's also not a war zone. Nairobi is a major African capital city with modern neighborhoods, malls, restaurants, and tech hubs. And then it's also right next to areas that you probably shouldn't wander into. Now, if you understand that balance, Kenya can be a great experience. If you ignore it, well, that's when people tend to get into trouble. It's really no different than when I lived in the Philippines. The Philippines, I never really felt unsafe, but there are certainly some areas that I wouldn't walk into at nighttime. Uh, and the same goes here in Kenya. So the first thing I want to talk about is crime in Nairobi. What exactly is the reality? Most crime affecting Nairobi is nonviolent crime. This is the crime you need to worry about. It has been repeated over and over to me by literally every Kenyon who sees that you have a nice phone. and that is do not walk outside holding your expensive phone just walking on the sidewalk because somebody on a motorbike is going to come up and snatch it and ride off into the uh sunset. And that is the reality. So you have to be aware of your surroundings when you have your phone out. You either look real quick, stick it back in your pocket, zip up um or do what I have, a pack safe travel sling bag that locks, or uh don't pull it out at all. And so it's not a violent crime, but it happens to Kenyons as well. And Kenyons will be the first to tell you, don't walk around with your phone out of your pocket. And so that's probably really like the major thing that you got to worry about here. pickpocketing, phone snatching, bag grabs, basically opportunistic theft. You know, if there's an opportunity for theft, they probably will take it, including sunglasses or whatever. So, it is a good idea not to wear any expensive jewelry. I mean, I've got a $3 little Kenyon bracelet. I don't wear my normal gold chain that I normally wear. And I do have a Apple Watch. You know, I'll take that chance. I don't think anybody's going to rip a watch off of you. What they're looking for is something they can grab and take off like a purse or a backpack or phone that you're holding in your hand or a camera, which is why I have decided to mainly do my videos indoors, but then take B-roll footage with my phone later on to show you guys. Now, violent crime, it does exist, but tourists are rarely the target unless they're careless. So, for me, it kind of comes down to high-risk behaviors. High-risisk behaviors, not really places. So, I would say the biggest risk factor isn't the city, it's the behavior. So, the most common mistakes that I see and hear about walking at night alone. That's a big no no. Using your phone openly on the street, wearing expensive watches or jewelry, wandering around unfamiliar neighborhoods. Really in Kenya, you should stick to places like Kilamani, Westlands, Karen. There are some others as well. Getting drunk and losing awareness. If you do those things, then Nairobi will punish you. But again, the safer areas for travels, and I will mark them down on the screen here in case you're wanting to come out and stay like this place here is in the area called Kilamani. So you got Westlands, Kilamani, Lavington, Karen, parts of Upper Hill. Those are pretty much the areas that you should really, if you're going to reside or even stay in a hotel. And during the day, you're safe to walk. Just don't pull your phone out. Don't worry about a bunch of expensive stuff. you'll you'll be more than fine. I've already walked around. I've went to a local market to buy a hoodie and it was there was no foreigners. It was all Kenyans on these little uh shops along the street and hey, no, no problem. So, those places I mentioned are relatively safe, especially during the day and early evening as long as you use common sense. And I think that really goes with almost anywhere you travel, including the United States. There's a lot of places in the United States I wouldn't step into even during the daytime. And the second thing I want to talk about is scams that you need to know. And the most common ones are actually pretty simple. Taxi scams. Unofficial taxis overcharging. Drivers claiming your destination is closed. Fake ride hailing drivers trying to wave you over, hey, where you going? You need a ride? Stick to Uber and Bolt. And Uber is probably the preferred one. and you can connect your card um or in my case I connected my PayPal so I never have to deal with cash. Pay them, boom, I'm done. I don't need to carry a lot of cash with me in Kenya. But if you stick to Uber and Bolt, period, you'll be fine. Uh you got to watch out for street help scams. Someone helping you cross the street. I I not at that point in my life where I need somebody to help me cross the street, but if you are like that, offering directions and then demanding money. Stick to asking uh mall staff like security guards and things like that questions like that distracting you while another person steals. Again, I highly recommend my packs safe sling bag. All locks up cross, wear it in front, everything's locked. They can't snatch it and they can't cut into it as well. So, I do highly recommend this. It's on my website, geo onthego.com. Click on Geo's travel essentials for all the gear I recommend for traveling or living anywhere in the world really to be honest, but in particular warmer climates. And really the solution to this is just be polite, but be firm and keep moving. Don't stop and engage. Just keep moving. And you're going to get some beggars as well. Uh to be honest, the Philippines was getting more aggressive, much more aggressive than here. here, they might come up and ask you, but in the Philippines, the baja, they would be grabbing on my pockets and stuff and that walking into the ATM uh booths with you just really invasive and the security did nothing. But here, they just really ask you and you just say, "I'm sorry, I have no cash. I have no cash." Just ignore them and they move on. Usually happens outside of the malls. Now, police shakedowns, they're rare, uh but they do happen. And if you're, you know, in Southeast Asia, you know, those things happen as well. Asking for ID, claiming a minor violation, hinting at a fine on the spot, and basically stay calm, respectful, and don't argue, and never escalate. Okay. Now, here's the part I got a lot of comments on in the comment section of my last video, and that is health and HIV. So, let's get honest here. And I think one of the big mistakes is that everybody seems to group Africa all together. Listen, that's a huge continent with 53 countries. We're talking Kenya in particular. So that's what I'm going to talk about. Yes, Kenya has a higher HIV rate than Western countries. That's a fact. I don't think anybody will argue that. HIV reality for travelers though, HIV is not a casual contact risk. You don't get it from restaurants, handshakes, transportation, hotels. I I mean, people should know this by now that that stuff just it doesn't happen. The risk comes from unprotected sex. If you meet a Kenyan girl and you like, you're going to use protection, you know? I mean, just like you would in any country. I don't know why you would take the risk otherwise. And if you don't and you want to enter into a relationship with that girl, you can go down to the pharmacy and get home kit, test each other right in the privacy of your own home together. You're both negative and then you're free to to do whatever you want. Or you can go down to a clinic and get a full battery of tests for very inexpensive and uh you know, then you're fine. It doesn't have to be I don't know why people make such a big deal out of it. As long as you're not going around uh having sex with every Kenyan girl unprotected, then you're going to be fine. But again, it comes from unprotected sex, multiple partners, alcohol field bad decisions, right? The uncomfortable truth is if you're sleeping around, ignoring protection, and assuming it won't happen to you, and that's going to be risky anywhere in the world, right? Not just Kenya. So, the smart approach, like I mentioned earlier, if you're dating or intimate with someone, use protection. Get tested either at a clinic or buy a HomeKit, don't rush into anything, and avoid transactional situations. And if you if you're going into that, that's risky again anywhere in the world. And if you do those things, then then your risk is going to drop dramatically. And listen, Kenyons are are very much aware of HIV, too. And they are also very cautious. They're they're not going to just jump into wanting unprotected sex as well. So if you get into a relationship with a Kenyan girl, guess what? She's probably before even you mention it is going to say, "Listen, if you want unprotected sex, we need to go take a test." They're very well educated in that manner. Okay, so moving on. I'm not going to get into numbers and all of that kind of stuff. You guys can look it up yourself and and figure things out. How to stay safe in Nairobi. Some simple rules that will work. And if you follow those rules, then that's going to eliminate 90% of your problems. Don't walk at night. Take your Uber and your Bolt and you're fine. I've been doing that the first trip when I was out here 3 weeks. I've been out here about 3 weeks now. I've been doing the same thing. There's no issues. There's a big expat community here, believe it or not. A big one. And they have zero issues, too, because they follow these simple rules. Again, use Uber and Bolt. Don't walk at night. Keep your phone discreet. Dress low-key. I'm not dressing up here in Bangkok. I dressed up a little nicer, but here I don't. Stay in the good neighborhoods, the ones I'm the ones I mentioned earlier. Trust your instincts. It isn't paranoia. It's just smart travel in my opinion. So, listen, mindset matters and if you follow these, Nairobi is going to reward you big time. Awareness, confidence, respect, and it's going to punish arrogance, carelessness, and being naive. So the final verdict in my opinion is Kenya safe. Kenya in Nairobi specifically is safe enough for smart travelers and dangerous for careless ones. And again this could be said for any place in the world, right? If you're responsible, aware, and respectful, you can have an amazing experience here. And and I have I've had an amazing experience and I'm going to continue to have an amazing experience with dating, with traveling, with the nature, with everything. In fact, uh, this Sunday I'm meeting up with a viewer of all things. You know, who who knew I would have a viewer in Nairobi, but I've had a few reach out to me. And, uh, one wants to meet up and and they all, uh, play pickle ball. Now, I've never played pickle ball, but I will join and, uh, try to learn the game. And, and this group of expats, uh, play at the uh, Two Rivers Mall on Wednesdays and Sundays, they have pickle ball. Um, great way to meet other expats and stuff. But again, Nairobi can be fantastic, guys. Kenya can be fantastic. But if you treat it like Disney World, you're going to be rolling the dice. So guys, if you want more real talk about safety, travel, dating, and living abroad, subscribe to the channel, hit that like button, click the bell notification so you're notified every time I come out with a new video. And if you're planning a trip and you want personal guidance either to Southeast Asia, Kenya, check the links below for my consultations and travel resources. And I will see you guys in the next one. [music] [music] Pack your bags, [music] don'tesitate, let's go before it's too late. Time keeps slipping away [music] from us. Keep living like this.

videos.transcript: a picnic is a great opportunity to catch up with loved ones and family many people are now preferring picnic areas of hotels and restaurants as a place to spend time with their loved ones today I'll share with you some of the topic areas in and near Nairobi before we continue I would like to welcome you to my channel if this is your first time here you are most welcome remember to subscribe and hit the notification Bell so that you are notified every time I upload a new video to the returning subscribers welcome back number one is ololua nature trail orolua Trail is located in the leafy suburbs of Karen the Bugatti river flows through ololua Forest the forest provides visitors with fantastic picnic spots for groups entrance fee is 200 Kenyan shillings for adults and 100 Kenya Shillings for children it is open from 9am to 6 PM daily number two is Nairobi Botanical Garden this one is located at Nairobi National Museum which is one and a half kilometers from Nairobi Central business district it is a project of the national museums of Kenya seeking to showcase the diversity of vegetation in Kenya entry to the museum is 200 Kenyan shillings for adults and 100 Kenya Shillings for children below 16 years Kenyan residents entry to the Garden is free [Music] number three is Nairobi Mamba Village this is East Africa's largest crocodile farm located off langata Karen Road it is one of the best picnic spots in Nairobi because it provides visitors with a range of activities like boat riding camel riding or shredding and many more on-site also are tense for camping it is open daily from 9am to 6 pm entrance fee is 200 Kenya Shillings for adults number four is olod tepes picnic site it is located in kiserian about one hour's drive from Nairobi Central business district here you get a beautiful view of gong Hills we have a restaurant on site this place is very ideal for picnic or even an overnight camping trip it is open for 24 hours daily [Music] number five is Natale Gardens this one is located along keraton Drive gong they have a lovely large aqua blue pool a fountain waterfall and bouncing castles for the kids it is advisable to make a booking if you would like to organize a picnic for more than five people the entrance fee is 400 Kenyan shillings for adults and 200 Kenya Shillings for kids it is open daily from 9am to 5 PM number six is August 7th Memorial Park Museum it is located at intersection of Moy Avenue and ilesalasi Avenue Nairobi it is a site of the 1998 U.S embassy bombing which was donated to the August 7th Memorial trust which converted into a park it features a beautifully landscaped Garden sculptures and so much more it is an ideal picnic site it is open Monday to Saturday from 9am to 6 pm and Sunday and public holidays from 1 pm to 6 pm entrance fee is 30 Kenya Shillings number seven is Karen blixen Museum it is situated at the foot of the gong Isles in Karen there is a small forest with nature trails for a refreshing walk and the grounds are suitable for a picnic it is ideal for history and nature lovers Out of Africa an oscar-winning film based on current life story put the Farmers on the international map the entrance fee is 200 Kenyan shillings for adults and 100 Kenya Shillings for kids below 16 years it is open daily from 10 AM to 6 pm including weekends and public holidays [Music] number eight is City Park the nature trails in this park are popular with nature levels who come to escape the city's noise the Open Spaces in the park attract many people who love picnics the Park is open for free to the public but the public toilets in there are charged [Music] number nine is divaji Garden jivaji is located in Nairobi Central business district the park is adored with beautiful trees and Gardens providing the shade you'll need while here it has beautifully crafted benches and sculptures adding to its beauty the park is an ideal picnic spot and its location makes it accessible to everyone in the city entrance is free of charge number 10 is karura Forest Reserve it is located in the outskirts of Nairobi in kiambu here you can get Scenic nature trails caves archaeological sites and well-maintained cycling trails a tour guide is available to help you get a better understanding of the forest to get the best out of your picnic you can set your picnic by the rocks in the river facing the falls and enjoy every moment picnics are charged at 150 Kenya Shillings for adults and 100 Kenya Shillings for children under 12 fears it is open daily from 6 30 a.m to 6 pm number 11 is waterfalls in tingoni this site is located in tingoni limuru kiambo County entry fee is 450 per adult and 200 Kenya Shillings per child it is set on a hill with a view of nairobi's skyline it is open daily from 8 A.M to 6 pm number 12 is langata Botanical Gardens this one is located 20 kilometers from Nairobi Central business district and less than 20 minutes from current Shopping Center it is ideal for relaxation inspiration celebration and a therapeutic vibe Panda secreted by natural vegetation are available here for privacy entrance fee is 500 Kenya Shillings for adults it is open daily from 8 AM to 7pm [Music] number 13 is Paradise Lost Paradise Lost is located in the outskirts of Nairobi in kiambu Road this park offers an unforgettable picnic experience on location arcades waterfalls nature trails large rocks and so many more the beautiful scenery and surrounding nature makes it a perfect place for a picnic get away the park only accepts mobile money payments which is worth nothing too entrance fee is 400 Kenya Shillings per adult and 300 Kenya Shillings for children if you think public transport matatu's number 100 from City Center will take you there number 14 is Evergreen Park Evergreen Park is located in kiambu Road 500 meters to the left over the northern bypass overpass time spent for picnic here is all worth it activities here are fishing in the man-made Dam us and camel riding bouncing castle for kids and so much more entry fee is 500 Kenya Shillings per adult and 400 Kenyan shillings per child 3 to 11 years each activity costs 100 Kenya Shillings it is open daily from 8 AM to 6 pm number 15 is Honorable John mituki Memorial Conservation Park it is located along the Nairobi River from the former Globe roundabout to keep on the road the beautification process involved removal of tons of garbage landscaping and tree planting it is open daily from 6 a.m to 6 pm no entrance fee is charged thank you so much for watching this video till the end remember to like and leave a comment in the comment section see you in my next one [Music] thank you [Music] foreign [Music]
